Ok propose:

1. Check if /etc/security/pwquality.conf exists. If not: create empty file with newline:

   run_cmd touch /etc/security/pwquality.conf
   run_cmd printf '\n' >> /etc/security/pwquality.conf

2. backup: run_cmd cp /etc/security/pwquality.conf /etc/security/pwquality.conf.bak

3. Ensure correct permissions: run_cmd chmod 0644 /etc/security/pwquality.conf; run_cmd chown root:root /etc/security/pwquality.conf

4. Use sed to delete any existing lines:

   run_cmd sed -i -E '/^\s*#?\s*enforce_for_root\b/d' /etc/security/pwquality.conf

5. Append canonical entry:

   run_cmd echo 'enforce_for_root = 1' >> /etc/security/pwquality.conf
